mnemonics: Proof of Work: Powerful Owners Wait (for validation)
Byzantine Generals: Loyal Officers Vote Reliably Against Treason
keyPoints: Consensus is the mechanism that allows a decentralized network to agree on the state of the ledger.
Proof of Work (mining) and Proof of Stake (validating) are the two most common consensus algorithms.
The goal is to achieve Byzantine Fault Tolerance, meaning the network can agree even if some participants are malicious or faulty.
commonMistakes: Thinking consensus is about speed; it's primarily about security and agreement, not transaction speed.
Believing Proof of Work and Proof of Stake are the only algorithms; others like Proof of Authority and Delegated Proof of Stake also exist.
Assuming a 51% attack means an attacker can steal coins; it primarily allows them to reverse their own transactions and double-spend.
